topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The phone slipped from my grasp, hitting the hardwood floor with a sharp crack that echoed in the silence of the living room. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in my throat, the image of a profile I’d never seen before burning itself into my vision. It was her. My daughter, Maya, but this wasn't the Maya I knew—the quiet, bookish girl who barely spoke unless forced. This account was vibrant, filled with carefully curated images and cryptic, almost aggressive captions. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, a heavy, unpleasant pressure. I felt my jaw clench so tightly my teeth ached. I didn't know where to begin, only that the carefully constructed facade of my family was suddenly, terribly, cracked open.

[story 2]

I watched her scroll through the feed, the silence of the house amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. Every notification was a tiny pinprick of alarm. I moved toward her bedroom, my footsteps unnaturally loud on the stairs. When I finally knocked, the sound felt like an accusation. When the door opened, she didn't look up immediately, her posture stiff, defensive. I saw the subtle flicker in her eyes—a quick dart away, a practiced evasion. "What is this?" The question came out sharper than I intended, laced with a brittle edge. She finally met my gaze, and the resistance in her expression was palpable, a wall rising between us.

[story 3]

It started with a misplaced login detail, a forgotten password that suddenly yielded a world I hadn't anticipated. The discovery hit me like a physical blow, stealing the air from my lungs. I paced the kitchen floor, my movements jerky and agitated, unable to settle. Every thought was sharp, accusatory, circling the implications of what I’d found. When I confronted her later, the air in the room grew thick and charged. Her response was measured, almost too calm, a deliberate attempt to deflect the scrutiny. I stood by the counter, arms crossed so tightly my knuckles were white, the tension radiating off me in palpable waves.
